label: New
items: Up to 5 photos per Plant Lens identification — better angle coverage, dramatically more accurate species ID. Multi-ID stays at one overview photo by design.

Pl@ntNet is now the primary plant identifier, with Gemini cross-checking. Pl@ntNet's botanical database beats general AI vision on species accuracy; Gemini still handles care guides, health, disease, pest and suggested tasks.
Tag each photo's organ (leaf / flower / fruit / bark) by tapping the chip under the thumbnail — improves Pl@ntNet's confidence and lets it short-circuit the AI ID round-trip on confident matches.

label: Improved
items: Identification results show a provenance pill — "Pl@ntNet", "Pl@ntNet + AI agreed", "Pl@ntNet (AI disagreed)", or "AI only" — so you can see which engine drove the answer.
When Pl@ntNet and the AI disagree on the species, both names are surfaced side by side so you can verify against the photo before saving.
Plant Lens History now shows a "+N" badge on the thumbnail when a session used multiple photos, and expands into a grid view.
Fixed: Multi-ID and Analyse sessions were silently failing the History table's action constraint — both now persist correctly.
